author: maker-p1
text: Love this. Spotify's discovery is an echo chamber — it reinforces the taste you already have instead of expanding it into new artists.

author: maker-p2
text: The killer feature would be adoption tracking. Most discovery never converts into a lasting habit — a first play is not a win.

author: maker-p3
text: A comfort-to-curiosity dial is genius. The biggest gap in every recommender is control over how far the recs stretch.

author: maker-p4
text: As an aspirational explorer I save tons of new music and forget all of it. Retention and rotation are the real unmet need.

author: maker-p5
text: Guided genre missions — get into jazz over 3 weeks with a staged on-ramp — would be a game changer for beginners.

author: maker-p6
text: Grounded 'why this' bridges build trust. Recommendations without a reason feel random and I never act on them.

author: maker-p7
text: Busy parents need zero-effort discovery that respects their time and comes to them instead of making them dig.

author: maker-p8
text: Stuck-in-a-loop is so real. People fall back on the same songs on repeat; the product that breaks that rut wins.
